data.headline: Basement Finishing in Greenwich, CT
data.subheadline: Licensed Connecticut basement finishing contractor serving Greenwich and Fairfield County , complete transformations from unfinished to move-in ready.

data.title: Basement Finishing in Greenwich, CT
data.content:
Greenwich homes often have large, unfinished basements with high ceilings and significant square footage , 800 to 2,000+ square feet of potential living space. Many estates in Belle Haven, Riverside, and Old Greenwich have basements that serve as storage but could be transformed into home theaters, wine cellars, guest suites, home offices, or recreation rooms.

BuiltWell provides full-service basement finishing in Greenwich with the quality and attention to detail that homeowners here expect. We handle every trade , framing, electrical, plumbing, HVAC, insulation, drywall, flooring, and finish work , under one contract.

data.title: What Is Included in a Basement Finishing Project in Greenwich
data.content:
A full basement finishing project in Greenwich covers every trade from waterproofing assessment through final trim: moisture management, framing, electrical, plumbing, HVAC extension, insulation, drywall, flooring, painting, and all finish work. Greenwich has a broad mix of pre-war estates and mid-century homes, and foundation types range from cut fieldstone in older properties to poured concrete in postwar construction, with ceiling heights that can vary significantly across both.

We start with a thorough assessment of the foundation walls, floor slab, moisture levels, ceiling height, mechanical system locations, and structural elements. In Greenwich, older fieldstone and block foundations often show lateral pressure cracks and mortar erosion, and many pre-war basements have low clearance around beam pockets that needs to be mapped before the framing plan is drawn. We look for signs of past seepage at the perimeter and around any floor drains before committing to the layout.

Framing includes steel or wood stud walls with rigid foam insulation on the foundation walls. Electrical scope covers new circuits, recessed lighting, outlet and switch placement, and panel upgrades when needed. Plumbing includes bathroom rough-in where applicable, wet bar connections, and sump pump assessment. The varied ceiling heights found in many Greenwich basements affect recessed light placement and duct routing, so we work that out during planning rather than in the field.

Flooring options include LVP, tile, or engineered hardwood rated for below-grade installation. HVAC extension brings heating and cooling to the finished space. All projects include drywall, painting, trim, interior doors, and a final walkthrough. Greenwich projects frequently incorporate home office built-ins or a dedicated gym layout, and we plan the electrical and lighting circuits for those uses from the start rather than adding circuits after drywall is closed.

data.title: What Makes Basement Finishing in Greenwich Different
data.content:
Greenwich basement projects tend to be larger in scope and higher in finish quality than typical Connecticut basements. Home theaters with dedicated sound systems, wine storage rooms with climate control, and guest suites with full bathrooms are common requests. Many Greenwich homes also have walkout basements that offer natural light and direct outdoor access , expanding the design possibilities significantly.

Backcountry Greenwich properties on well and septic systems may have specific drainage considerations. Waterfront properties in Riverside and Old Greenwich may experience elevated water table conditions that require robust waterproofing before finishing can begin.

data.title: Greenwich Building Department and Permits
data.content: The Greenwich Building Department at 101 Field Point Road requires permits for basement finishing projects involving framing, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC work. Egress window installations require both building and zoning review. Review times in Greenwich typically run 2-4 weeks. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection.

Ranges are illustrative for typical Connecticut projects. Final pricing depends on scope, materials, site conditions, and permit fees. Detailed written estimate provided before any work begins.

data.title: Why Basement Finishing in Greenwich Requires Local Expertise
data.content:
Greenwich homes present basement conditions that contractors without local experience regularly miss.

Greenwich basements in homes built before 1970 may have stone or block foundations with active moisture intrusion that needs to be resolved before finishing. We assess every Greenwich basement for moisture conditions, foundation integrity, and drainage performance before proposing a finishing plan.

The upgraded expectations in Greenwich mean that basement finishes need to match the quality of the home's main living spaces. We deliver that level of craftsmanship consistently , from recessed lighting placement to trim detail.

data.title: Our Basement Finishing Process
data.subtitle: Every basement finishing project follows the same five-step process , structured to keep projects on schedule, on budget, and clearly communicated throughout.

steps.title: Assessment
steps.description: We visit your home and evaluate the basement , foundation condition, moisture levels, ceiling height, mechanical systems, drainage, and structural elements. We identify what needs to be addressed before finishing and what the space can become. No charge. No obligation.

steps.title: Design & Proposal
steps.description: You receive a layout design and detailed written proposal covering every trade: waterproofing, framing, electrical, plumbing, HVAC, insulation, drywall, flooring, and finish work. Each line item is broken out so you understand where the budget is going.

steps.title: Permits & Prep
steps.description: We submit permit applications, order materials, and prepare the basement for construction. Any waterproofing or moisture remediation happens first, before framing begins. We coordinate all inspections throughout the project.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction follows a structured sequence: waterproofing, framing, rough electrical and plumbing, insulation, drywall, flooring, trim, paint, and fixtures. You receive daily updates and a clean workspace throughout.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk through the finished space together , every wall, every outlet, every trim piece, every finish detail. If anything needs attention, we address it before calling the project complete.

data.title: Project Timeline
data.subtitle: Most basement finishing projects take six to fourteen weeks from signed proposal to final walkthrough.

items.title: Planning & Design
items.description: 1–2 Weeks. Consultation, measurements, layout design, and detailed proposal.

items.title: Permits & Materials
items.description: 2–4 Weeks. Permit applications, material ordering, and scheduling.

items.title: Construction
items.description: 4–8 Weeks. Waterproofing, framing, rough-ins, insulation, drywall, flooring, and finishes.

items.title: Final Touches
items.description: 1 Week. Trim, paint, hardware, final inspections, and walkthrough.

items.question: How long does basement finishing take in Greenwich?
items.answer: Most Greenwich basement finishing projects take six to fourteen weeks from signed proposal to final walkthrough. A basic finish (framing, drywall, flooring, paint) takes 6-8 weeks. A full finish with bathroom, egress windows, and upgraded features takes 10-14 weeks. Your written proposal includes a complete project schedule.

items.question: How much does basement finishing cost in Greenwich?
items.answer: Basement finishing in Greenwich typically ranges from $25,000 for a basic 400-600 sq ft finish to $180,000+ for a upscale walkout suite with full bathroom, wet bar, and upgraded finishes. A mid-range project with bathroom and quality finishes generally falls between $40,000 and $70,000. Your written proposal includes a detailed cost breakdown by trade.

items.question: Do I need permits for basement finishing in Greenwich?
items.answer: Yes. Basement finishing in Greenwich requires permits from the Greenwich Building Department for framing, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC work. Egress windows , required for any basement bedroom , need additional review. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection.

items.question: Do I need to waterproof my basement before finishing in Greenwich?
items.answer: Not always, but moisture must be assessed and managed. We test moisture levels in every Greenwich basement before proposing a finishing plan. If active moisture intrusion is present, it must be resolved before framing and drywall installation. Some basements need drainage improvements, sump pump installation, or exterior waterproofing. Others simply need vapor barriers and proper insulation. We assess honestly and recommend only what's necessary.

items.question: Can I add a bathroom to my finished basement in Greenwich?
items.answer: Yes. Adding a bathroom is one of the most common basement finishing upgrades. The feasibility depends on your existing drain line depth and location. Many Greenwich homes have drain lines that can accommodate a basement bathroom. If the existing drain is above the bathroom location, an ejector pump system is installed. We evaluate drain conditions during the initial assessment and include bathroom scope in the proposal.
